title: crepis multicaulis subsp. congesta
intro: Crepis multicaulis subsp. congesta, also known as congested hawk's beard or rough hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and roots are also rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, offering various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting immune function. Crepis multicaulis subsp. congesta is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive problems, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional before consumption.
